name: MCP News Feed
description: News about MCP, AI agents, MCP servers, tool integrations, security, developer tooling, and enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: Intellibooks Guide: The 5 Layers of Agent Memory That Make Enterprise AI Agents Smarter
articles.summary: Artificial Intelligence is rapidly moving beyond simple chatbots into intelligent AI agents capable of planning, reasoning, learning, and completing complex business tasks. But what separates an enterprise-grade AI agent from a basic language model? The answer lies in memory. At Intellibooks, we...
articles.author: Intellibooks AI

articles.title: MCP Authentication: How We Secured 12 MCP Servers Without Losing Our Minds
articles.summary: TL;DR: MCP authentication is genuinely more complex than regular API auth because you're managing credentials across many servers, for many agents, under many user identities - often all at once. The approaches range from static API keys (fast, insecure at scale) to OAuth 2.1 with PKCE...
articles.author: Sahajmeet Kaur

articles.title: MCP Governance: What It Actually Means in Production (And the Four Walls We Had to Build)
articles.summary: TL;DR: MCP governance is the set of controls that determine which agents can access which tools, under which identities, with what limits, and with what audit trail. Raw MCP has none of this - it's a protocol for structured tool calls, not a policy engine. The governance layer is something teams...
articles.author: Sahajmeet Kaur

articles.title: What Is an MCP Registry? (And the NxM Problem It Solves)
articles.summary: TL;DR: An MCP registry is a centralized catalog of MCP servers - what they do, how to connect, what tools they expose, and who's authorized to call them. Without one, every developer and agent maintains their own copy of that information, credential rotation breaks everything, and access control is...
articles.author: Sahajmeet Kaur

articles.title: Designing tools that AI can actually use
articles.summary: Here's a tool definition I see all the time: @tool def get_data(id: str): """Gets data.""" ... The model has no way to know when to call this. "Gets data" could match almost any request. And when an agent is choosing among thirty tools, the description does most of the routing. To the model, that...
articles.author: Wojciech Wentland

articles.summary: I use Duolingo every day for Japanese. I genuinely like it. But three things drive me crazy. The progression is one-size-fits-all. The learning sequence is the same for everyone. articles.title: 1024-token RAG chunks cut my storage cost in half — and nearly doubled my Claude bill
articles.summary: Switching from 512 to 1024-token chunks saved $1.20/month on Vectorize. It cost me $92 more on Claude Sonnet. I didn't see that coming until I did the math. articles.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
articles.summary: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
